disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe generalised pruritus with a flare of dry, red, scaly plaques in a flexural distribution, on a background of lifelong atopy, triggered by cold dry weather and a recent viral upper respiratory infection
trigger_question_examples: Where is the rash and what does it look like?
Have you had skin trouble like this before?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've had eczema all my life but this is a huge flare — the creases of my elbows and knees are red, scaly and weeping, and the itch is unbearable. It kicked off after the cold snap and a head cold last week.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Profound sleep deprivation (48 hours) and acute psychological distress driven by the itch-scratch cycle
trigger_question_examples: How is this affecting your sleep?
How are you coping with it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I haven't slept in two days because of the itching. I'm exhausted and honestly at the end of my tether.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Barrier breakdown — excoriation and serous weeping from scratching, raising the risk of secondary bacterial or viral infection
trigger_question_examples: Is any of the skin broken or weeping?
Have you scratched it until it bleeds or oozes?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — I've scratched some areas raw and they're weeping a clear fluid. The skin in the creases is cracked and open.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pertinent negatives that make secondary infection and eczema herpeticum unlikely at present — no honey-coloured crusting, no spreading hot erythema, no clustered punched-out blisters, afebrile, systemically well
trigger_question_examples: Are there any yellow crusts, hot spreading redness, or clusters of little blisters?
Do you have a fever or feel unwell in yourself?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No yellow crusts or golden scabs, no hot spreading redness, and no clusters of little punched-out blisters. I don't have a fever and I don't feel unwell in myself — it's really the itch and the weeping.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Over-the-counter 1% hydrocortisone has been ineffective and there is no regular emollient use; the patient is seeking a stronger prescription
trigger_question_examples: Are you using a moisturiser regularly?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've only got the chemist hydrocortisone and it's done nothing. I don't really use a moisturiser regularly. I think I need something stronger.
